concepts.title: The bar resolution and $H^n(G,M)$
concepts.blurb: The bar resolution gives a free $\mathbb{Z}[G]$-resolution of the trivial module $\mathbb{Z}$; cohomology of the dual cochain complex defines $H^n(G,M)=\mathrm{Ext}^n_{\mathbb{Z}[G]}(\mathbb{Z},M)$, the right derived functors of $M\mapsto M^G$.

concepts.title: $H^0$ as $G$-fixed points
concepts.blurb: Degree zero recovers the input: $H^0(G,M)=M^G$. Failure of right-exactness of $(-)^G$ is exactly what the higher $H^n$ correct, generating the long exact sequence $0\to A^G\to B^G\to C^G\xrightarrow{\delta} H^1(G,A)\to\cdots$.

concepts.title: $H^1$, crossed homomorphisms, and Hilbert 90
concepts.blurb: $H^1(G,M)$ is crossed homomorphisms $\varphi(gh)=\varphi(g)+g\varphi(h)$ modulo principal ones $g\mapsto gm-m$. Hilbert's Theorem 90 says $H^1(\mathrm{Gal}(L/K),L^\times)=1$ for cyclic $L/K$: every norm-one element is of the form $\sigma(\alpha)/\alpha$.

concepts.title: $H^2$ classifies group extensions
concepts.blurb: Equivalence classes of extensions $1\to A\to E\to G\to 1$ with prescribed $G$-action on abelian $A$ are in bijection with $H^2(G,A)$. Split extensions hit $0$; the nontrivial class assembles a section's failure-of-multiplicativity into the canonical 2-cocycle.

concepts.title: Cyclic groups, periodicity, and Tate cohomology
concepts.blurb: For $G=C_n$ the alternating $\sigma-1$/$N$-resolution gives a 2-periodic theory: $H^{2k}=M^G/N(M)$, $H^{2k+1}=\ker N/(\sigma-1)M$. Splicing in negative degrees defines Tate cohomology $\hat H^*$; the Herbrand quotient is multiplicative and a workhorse in class field theory.

concepts.title: Inflation–restriction and Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre
concepts.blurb: For $H\trianglelefteq G$ the five-term sequence $0\to H^1(G/H,M^H)\to H^1(G,M)\to H^1(H,M)^{G/H}\to H^2(G/H,M^H)\to H^2(G,M)$ is the low-degree edge of the LHS spectral sequence $E_2^{p,q}=H^p(G/H,H^q(H,M))\Rightarrow H^{p+q}(G,M)$.

concepts.title: Galois cohomology and the Brauer group
concepts.blurb: Continuous group cohomology of $G_k=\mathrm{Gal}(\bar k/k)$ acting on a Galois module $M$ is Galois cohomology $H^n(k,M)$. Hilbert 90 says $H^1(k,\bar k^\times)=0$; $H^2(k,\bar k^\times)=\mathrm{Br}(k)$ is the Brauer group of central simple algebras, with the Kummer sequence giving $H^1(k,\mu_n)=k^\times/(k^\times)^n$.
